reasoning: France have taken clear control as favorites, shortening to around +180 after reaching the final eight without conceding a goal in either knockout match, with Mbappe and Dembele in form and only Morocco standing before the semis. Spain are the strongest challenger: unbeaten in 35 straight matches and yet to concede all tournament, though their scoreless draw with Cape Verde flagged occasional finishing issues against a favorable path through Belgium. Argentina remain dangerous champions carried by a record-breaking Messi and elite knockout experience, but they have looked distinctly beatable, needing late 3-2 comebacks against both Cape Verde and Egypt, while England (Bellingham, Kane) carry talent but perennial mentality doubts and face a tricky Norway tie. Norway are the live dark horse, riding Haaland's seven goals and a Round-of-16 upset of Brazil, playing with freedom as underdogs against England.
